summary: Misleading SQL-access package ships Buffer polyfill and pulls undeclared-purpose dependency
details: The package name and description advertise SQL/database access, but index.js (the declared main entry) is a verbatim copy of the feross/buffer browser polyfill exporting `Buffer`. The README instructs users to install a different package name (`@sql-access/nods`), and a dead async stub `initializeDatabase` performs `await import('@sql-access/nodesql')` (a self-import) but is never invoked. No lifecycle hooks, no network I/O, no eval, and no obfuscation are present in the reachable code of this package. However, package.json declares a runtime dependency on `@sqlite-node/createsql`, which is never required by index.js and whose name does not correspond to a known legitimate SQL library — pulling an undeclared-purpose dependency into the installer's tree under a misleading package identity is the namespace-abuse shape. The dependency itself was not inspected here; its install-time and import-time behavior should be reviewed before this package is trusted.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
